question.text: What is the maximum subscription from any single investor (other than sponsor and related parties) in a REIT?

answer.text: Not more than 49% of total unit capital
Not more than 25% of total unit capital
Not more than 50% of total unit capital
Not more than 10% of total unit capital

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under REIT Regulations, the maximum subscription from any investor (other than sponsor(s), its related parties and its associates) should not be more than 25% of total unit capital. **The Trap:** This 25% cap prevents any single non-sponsor investor from dominating the REIT, ensuring diversified ownership. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The 25% maximum subscription applies to both InvITs and REITs, maintaining consistency in preventing concentration of ownership by non-sponsor investors.
